name: aztec-rise
description: The Mexica founded Tenochtitlan on an island in Lake Texcoco, beginning the rise of the Aztec Empire.

name: jewish-scapegoating-plague
description: Jews were scapegoated and massacred across Europe during the Black Death, with hundreds of communities destroyed.

name: black-death-demographic
description: The Black Death killed roughly one-third of Europe's population, transforming labor markets and social structures.

name: medici-banking
description: Giovanni di Bicci de' Medici founded the Medici Bank in Florence, launching one of history's most powerful dynasties.

name: insurance-origins
description: The earliest known marine insurance contract was written in Genoa, protecting cargo against sea risks.

name: swahili-kilwa-sultanate
description: The Kilwa Sultanate reached its apex as a Swahili trading power on the East African coast.

name: majapahit-maritime-trade
description: The Majapahit Empire dominated Southeast Asian maritime trade from its base in Java.

name: benin-edo-palace-system
friendly_name: Benin Edo Kingdom Palace Government System
description: The Benin Kingdom developed a sophisticated palace-based government under Oba Ewuare the Great.

name: wolof-empire-formation
description: The Jolof Empire unified several Wolof states in the Senegambia region of West Africa.

name: kilwa-gold-monopoly
friendly_name: Kilwa Controls East African Gold Trade
description: Kilwa Kisiwani monopolized the gold trade flowing from Great Zimbabwe mines to the Indian Ocean.

name: black-death-labor-shortage
friendly_name: Black Death Labor Shortage Transforms Europe
description: Massive plague deaths created severe labor shortages, giving surviving peasants unprecedented bargaining power.

name: labor-shortage-drives-wages
description: Post-plague labor scarcity drove wages sharply upward, prompting England's Statute of Labourers to cap pay.

name: peasant-revolts-increase
friendly_name: Peasant Revolts Surge in Medieval Europe
description: Post-plague tensions erupted in peasant uprisings including the French Jacquerie of 1358.

name: ayutthaya-founded
description: King Ramathibodi I founded the Kingdom of Ayutthaya, which became a major Siamese power for four centuries.

name: lannathai-unification
description: The Lanna Kingdom consolidated northern Thai territories under a unified Buddhist polity centered on Chiang Mai.

name: insurance-contract
description: The first known standalone insurance contract was issued in Genoa, covering a ship's cargo.

name: majapahit-empire
description: Under Prime Minister Gajah Mada, the Majapahit Empire reached its peak controlling much of maritime Southeast Asia.

name: letter-of-credit-development
description: Italian merchants developed letters of credit to facilitate long-distance trade without transporting currency.

name: hanseatic-league-dominance
description: The Hanseatic League of merchant guilds dominated Northern European trade from the Baltic to the North Sea.

name: ragusa-quarantine
description: Ragusa (Dubrovnik) enacted the first known quarantine law, requiring ships to isolate for 30 days before entry.

name: timur-empire-samarkand
friendly_name: Timur Establishes Empire with Capital at Samarkand
description: Timur (Tamerlane) established a vast empire from his capital at Samarkand spanning Central Asia to the Middle East.

name: peasants-revolt-1381
description: English peasants led by Wat Tyler marched on London to protest poll taxes and serfdom restrictions.

name: kongo-kingdom-founded
description: The Kingdom of Kongo was established in Central Africa along the Congo River with a centralized government.

name: joseon-dynasty
description: General Yi Seong-gye overthrew the Goryeo dynasty and founded Joseon, which ruled Korea for over 500 years.

name: medici-patronage
friendly_name: Medici Family Banking and Art Patronage
description: The Medici Bank was founded in Florence, funding both commerce and Renaissance art patronage.

name: medici-banking-innovations
description: The Medici Bank pioneered double-entry bookkeeping and branch banking across European cities.

name: banking-medici
description: Giovanni di Bicci de' Medici established the Medici Bank, which became Europe's most powerful financial institution.

name: mali-decline
description: The Mali Empire entered decline as provinces broke away and the Songhai Empire rose to challenge its dominance.

name: kilwa-sultanate
description: The Kilwa Sultanate reached peak power as East Africa's wealthiest trading state, minting its own coinage.

name: timurid-dynasty
description: The Timurid dynasty ruled Central Asia after Timur's death, fostering a golden age of Persian art and science.

name: venetian-republic
description: The Venetian Republic reached its zenith as a maritime trading empire controlling eastern Mediterranean commerce.

name: bill-of-exchange-development
description: Italian merchants refined bills of exchange as negotiable financial instruments for international trade.

name: spice-trade-dominance
description: Indian Ocean spice trade networks expanded, connecting Southeast Asian producers with European consumers.

name: mombasa-coastal-power
description: Mombasa grew into a major Swahili coast trading city, rivaling Kilwa in Indian Ocean commerce.

name: soga-kingdom
description: The Busoga kingdoms emerged in the Great Lakes region of present-day eastern Uganda.

name: fijian-culture
description: Fijian chieftdoms developed hierarchical political systems with fortified villages across the archipelago.

name: ottoman-timar-system
description: The Ottoman Empire used the timar system to grant land revenues to cavalry officers in exchange for military service.

name: import-tariffs-protection
description: European states began systematically using import tariffs to protect domestic industries from foreign competition.

name: somali-mogadishu-sultanate
friendly_name: Mogadishu Sultanate Flourishes on Somali Coast
description: The Mogadishu Sultanate prospered as a major Indian Ocean trading hub on the Somali coast.

name: buganda-kabaka-system
description: The Buganda kingdom developed its kabaka (king) system of centralized governance in the Great Lakes region.

name: kongo-kingdom-structure
description: The Kongo Kingdom formalized provincial governance under a manikongo (king) with appointed regional leaders.

name: great-lakes-kingdoms-system
friendly_name: Great Lakes Kingdoms Form Political System
description: Interlacustrine kingdoms including Buganda, Bunyoro, and Rwanda developed complex political hierarchies in East Africa.

name: salt-gold-trade-caravan
friendly_name: Salt and Gold Trade Dominates Trans-Saharan Routes
description: Trans-Saharan caravan routes thrived on exchanging Saharan salt for West African gold.

name: yap-stone-money-currency
description: The Yapese developed a unique currency of large limestone discs (rai stones) quarried from distant Palau.

name: maori-iwi-tribal-identity
description: Maori iwi (tribal) identities crystallized in New Zealand with distinct territories and political structures.

name: caravanserai-samarkand
description: Timur built an extensive caravanserai network around Samarkand to support Silk Road trade.

name: banking-credit-innovation
description: Italian banking houses expanded credit instruments, enabling large-scale international commerce.

name: insurance-maritime
description: Maritime insurance became standard practice in Mediterranean ports, reducing risk for long-distance traders.

name: malacca-sultanate
description: Parameswara founded the Malacca Sultanate, which became the dominant maritime power in Southeast Asia.

name: malacca-sultanate-founded
description: The Malacca Sultanate was founded on the Malay Peninsula, controlling the vital Strait of Malacca trade route.

name: le-dynasty-restoration
description: Le Loi defeated Ming Chinese forces and founded the Later Le dynasty, restoring Vietnamese independence.

name: khmer-decline
description: Ayutthaya forces sacked Angkor, accelerating the decline of the once-mighty Khmer Empire.

name: mutapa-empire
description: The Mutapa Empire succeeded Great Zimbabwe and controlled gold mining in southern Africa's interior.

name: bahmani-sultanate
description: The Bahmani Sultanate reached its zenith in the Deccan plateau of India before fragmenting into five states.

name: iroquois-confederacy
description: Five Haudenosaunee nations formed the Iroquois Confederacy under the Great Law of Peace.

name: oyo-confederacy-structure
description: The Oyo Empire formalized its political system with the Alaafin (king) balanced by the Oyo Mesi council.

name: rwanda-kingdom-formation
friendly_name: Rwanda Kingdom Emerges in Great Lakes
description: The Kingdom of Rwanda emerged in the Great Lakes region under a centralized monarchy.

name: rozwi-empire-rise
friendly_name: Rozwi Empire Rises After Great Zimbabwe
description: The Rozwi Empire rose to power in southern Africa under Changamire Dombo after Great Zimbabwe's decline.

name: inca-acllaconas
description: The Inca Empire institutionalized the acllaconas (chosen women) for religious, textile, and state functions.

name: haudenosaunee-confederacy
description: The Haudenosaunee Great Law of Peace united five nations in one of history's earliest federal democracies.

name: silk-road-decline
description: Ottoman conquest of Constantinople disrupted overland Silk Road trade, spurring European maritime exploration.

name: sulu-sultanate
description: The Sulu Sultanate was established in the southern Philippines as a center of Islamic trade and governance.

name: antwerp-stock-exchange
description: The Antwerp Bourse became the world's first purpose-built stock exchange for trading commodities and securities.

name: inca-quipu-standardized
description: The Inca Empire standardized quipu knotted-string records for census, taxation, and administrative data.

name: tahuantinsuyu-administrative
description: The Inca Empire organized Tahuantinsuyu into four suyus (quarters) with a hierarchical administrative system.

name: kazakh-khanate-zhanibek
description: Zhanibek and Kerey Khan broke from the Uzbek Khanate and founded the independent Kazakh Khanate.

name: venice-patent-system
description: Venice enacted the first codified patent law, granting inventors exclusive rights to their creations for ten years.

name: isabella-aragon
description: Isabella I became Queen of Castile, later uniting Spain with Ferdinand of Aragon to form a powerful monarchy.

name: kongo-kingdom-formation
description: The Kingdom of Kongo was formed in west-central Africa through conquest and alliance along the Congo River.

name: adil-shahi-dynasty
description: Yusuf Adil Shah founded the Adil Shahi dynasty, ruling the Bijapur Sultanate in the Deccan region of India.

name: barid-shahi-dynasty
description: The Barid Shahi dynasty established the Bidar Sultanate as one of the five Deccan successor states.

name: nizamshahi-dynasty
description: Ahmad Nizam Shah founded the Ahmadnagar Sultanate as a breakaway Deccan state from the Bahmani Sultanate.

name: songhai-askia-muhammad
description: Askia Muhammad seized power and expanded the Songhai Empire into the largest state in African history.

name: songhai-askia-reforms
description: Askia Muhammad reformed Songhai governance with provincial administration, Islamic courts, and standardized taxation.

name: columbian-exchange-disease
description: European diseases devastated Indigenous American populations after Columbus's voyages, killing millions.

name: deccan-sultanates
description: Five successor states of the Bahmani Sultanate ruled the Deccan plateau, fostering Indo-Islamic culture.

name: aztec-peak
description: The Aztec Empire reached peak power under Moctezuma II, ruling over 5 million people from Tenochtitlan.

name: columbian-exchange
description: Columbus's voyage initiated the transfer of plants, animals, and diseases between the Old and New Worlds.

name: olmec-civilization-founded
description: The Olmec, Mesoamerica's earliest major civilization, emerged along Mexico's Gulf Coast with monumental architecture.

name: maori-iwi-development
description: Maori iwi developed complex tribal structures with distinct territories, whakapapa lineages, and resource management.

name: buganda-kingdom
description: The Buganda Kingdom expanded around Lake Victoria through military conquest and strategic alliances.

name: luba-empire
friendly_name: Luba Empire Expands in Central Africa
description: The Luba Empire expanded in the Congo basin, establishing a sophisticated system of divine kingship.

name: zoo-ancient
description: Ancient Egyptian pharaohs maintained exotic animal collections as displays of power and divine authority.

name: embargo-trade-warfare
description: Mediterranean powers used trade embargoes as tools of economic warfare against rival states.

name: buganda-kabaka-succession
description: Buganda formalized royal succession rules for the kabaka, stabilizing political transitions in the kingdom.

name: kuba-kingdom-raffia-currency
friendly_name: Kuba Kingdom Uses Raffia as Currency
description: The Kuba Kingdom in the Congo basin used woven raffia cloth as a standardized currency for trade.

name: burundi-kingdom-establishment
friendly_name: Burundi Kingdom Established in Great Lakes
description: The Kingdom of Burundi was established in the Great Lakes region under a centralized mwami (king).

name: cowrie-shell-currency-africa
friendly_name: Cowrie Shells Used as Currency in African Trade
description: Cowrie shells from the Indian Ocean became a widespread currency across West African trade networks.

name: yap-political-hierarchy
friendly_name: Yap Political Hierarchy Based on Stone Wealth
description: Yapese chiefs derived political authority from ownership of rai stone money in Micronesia.

name: oneida-nation-iroquois
description: The Oneida Nation served as one of five founding members of the Haudenosaunee Confederacy.

name: population-collapse-americas
description: Smallpox and other Old World diseases caused catastrophic population decline among Indigenous Americans.

name: safavid-dynasty-founded
description: Shah Ismail I founded the Safavid dynasty, establishing Shia Islam as Persia's state religion.

name: safavid-shah-ismail
description: Shah Ismail conquered Tabriz and declared Twelver Shia Islam the official religion of Persia.

name: encomienda-system
description: Spain formalized the encomienda system, granting colonists control over Indigenous labor in the Americas.

name: qutb-shahi-dynasty
description: Sultan Quli founded the Qutb Shahi dynasty at Golconda, which became famous for its diamond trade.

name: ottoman-suleiman-magnificent
description: Suleiman the Magnificent began his 46-year reign, expanding the Ottoman Empire to its greatest extent.

name: mughal-babur-founds
description: Babur defeated the Delhi Sultanate at the Battle of Panipat and founded the Mughal Empire in India.

name: banjarmasin-sultanate
description: The Banjar Sultanate rose to power in southeastern Borneo as an Islamic trading state.

name: bankruptcy-law
description: England enacted the first statutory bankruptcy law under Henry VIII to regulate insolvent debtors.

name: molucca-spice-island-trade
description: European powers competed fiercely for control of the Moluccas, the world's sole source of cloves and nutmeg.

name: lunda-empire-formation
friendly_name: Lunda Empire Emerges in Central Africa
description: The Lunda Empire emerged in the southern Congo basin, expanding through trade and political alliances.

name: atlantic-slave-trade-forced
description: The transatlantic slave trade accelerated as Portuguese and Spanish demand for plantation labor surged.

name: silver-wealth-redistribution
description: Silver from Potosi and Mexican mines flooded global markets, causing inflation and shifting economic power.

name: ottoman-coffee-culture
description: The first coffeehouses opened in Istanbul, creating new spaces for social and intellectual exchange.

name: suleiman-kanuni-legal
description: Suleiman the Magnificent codified Ottoman secular law, earning the title 'Kanuni' (Lawgiver).

name: lithuanian-commonwealth
description: The Union of Lublin merged Poland and Lithuania into a single commonwealth, creating Europe's largest state.

name: safavid-mughal-relations
description: The Safavid and Mughal empires maintained complex diplomatic relations marked by rivalry over Kandahar.

name: mining-mercury-potosí
description: The mercury amalgamation process at Potosi dramatically increased silver output from South American mines.

name: akbar-administrative-reforms
description: Mughal Emperor Akbar implemented the mansabdari system and Todar Mal's land revenue reforms across India.

name: mataram-empire
description: The Mataram Sultanate rose to dominate most of Java under Senopati's leadership.

name: brunei-sultanate-expansion
description: The Brunei Sultanate expanded its influence across northern Borneo and parts of the Philippines.

name: abbas-great-reforms
friendly_name: Abbas I the Great Reforms Safavid State
description: Shah Abbas I modernized the Safavid military, centralized governance, and made Isfahan a magnificent capital.

name: toyotomi-hideyoshi-rule
description: Toyotomi Hideyoshi unified Japan after a century of civil war through military campaigns and diplomacy.

name: isfahan-capital-development
description: Shah Abbas I moved the Safavid capital to Isfahan, transforming it into one of the world's grandest cities.

name: mycenaean-civilization
description: Mycenaean civilization emerged in mainland Greece with fortified palaces and Linear B script.

name: benguela-slave-market
friendly_name: Benguela Established as Slave Trade Center
description: The Portuguese established Benguela in Angola as a major embarkation point for the transatlantic slave trade.

name: tswana-kingdoms
description: Tswana-speaking chieftains consolidated kingdoms across present-day Botswana and South Africa.

name: aceh-sultanate
description: The Aceh Sultanate in northern Sumatra reached peak power under Sultan Iskandar Muda.

name: hawaiian-kingdoms
description: Hawaiian ali'i (chiefs) established competing kingdoms across the islands with complex social hierarchies.

name: corporation-charter
description: England chartered the East India Company, one of the first major joint-stock trading corporations.

name: oyo-empire-expansion
friendly_name: Oyo Empire Expands in West Africa
description: The Oyo Empire expanded across present-day Nigeria using its powerful cavalry forces.

name: yoruba-oyo-confederation
description: The Oyo confederation united Yoruba city-states under an alaafin (king) with tributary relationships.

name: lunda-empire
description: The Lunda Empire achieved regional dominance in Central Africa through trade networks and political expansion.

name: kuba-kingdom
description: The Kuba Kingdom flourished in the Congo under King Shyaam, known for sophisticated art and governance.

name: old-age-care
description: The Netherlands established some of Europe's earliest purpose-built old age homes for elderly citizens.

name: joint-stock-company-development
description: The Dutch East India Company (VOC) became the first publicly traded joint-stock company.

name: polish-lithuanian-golden-age
description: The Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth entered its golden age as Europe's largest and most tolerant state.

name: venetian-trade-decline
description: Venice's trade dominance declined as Atlantic routes bypassed the Mediterranean and Ottoman power grew.

name: hanseatic-league-decline
description: The Hanseatic League declined as nation-states and Dutch merchants eroded its Northern European trade monopoly.

name: dutch-golden-age-trade
description: The Dutch Republic launched its Golden Age of trade with the founding of the VOC and global commercial networks.

name: srivijaya-decline
description: The Srivijaya maritime empire fully dissolved after centuries of decline, succeeded by Majapahit and Malacca.

name: lunda-expansion-network
friendly_name: Lunda Empire Expands Through Trade Networks
description: The Lunda Empire expanded through long-distance trade in copper, salt, and slaves across Central Africa.

name: agricultural-commercialization
description: Western European agriculture shifted toward commercial production for markets rather than subsistence farming.

name: joint-stock-company-formation
description: The English East India Company was chartered, pioneering the joint-stock corporate model for global trade.

name: tobacco-addiction-global
description: Tobacco from the Americas spread worldwide, becoming a major global commodity and source of addiction.

name: spice-monopoly-dutch
friendly_name: Dutch Spice Monopoly in East Indies
description: The Dutch VOC established a monopoly over the spice trade in the East Indies through force and treaties.

name: teak-timber-trade
friendly_name: Teak Timber Trade from Southeast Asia
description: Southeast Asian teak became a vital commodity traded to European and Asian shipbuilders.

name: indigo-trade-expands
description: Indian indigo dye exports surged as European textile demand grew, reshaping global trade patterns.

name: cocoa-chocolate-commodity
description: Cacao from Mesoamerica became a luxury drink in European courts after Spanish colonizers exported it.

name: edo-period
description: Tokugawa Ieyasu established the shogunate at Edo, beginning over 250 years of peace and isolation in Japan.
